# [CHECKLIST TYPE] Checklist: [FEATURE NAME]

**Purpose**: [Brief description of what this checklist validates]
**Created**: [DATE]
**Feature**: [Link to spec.md or relevant documentation]
**Stack**: Next.js · React · Node.js · Express · SQLite → PostgreSQL

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

<!--
  ============================================================================
  IMPORTANT: The checklist items below are SAMPLE ITEMS for illustration only.

  The /speckit-checklist command MUST replace these with actual items based on:
  - User's specific checklist request
  - Feature requirements from spec.md
  - Technical context from plan.md
  - Implementation details from tasks.md

  DO NOT keep these sample items in the generated checklist file.

-->

## Feature Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are all acceptance criteria from spec.md addressed and unambiguous?
- [ ] CHK002 Are loading, empty, error, and success UI states defined for every async operation?
- [ ] CHK003 Are edge cases and boundary conditions covered in requirements?
- [ ] CHK004 Are all user-facing flows (happy path + alternate paths) specified?
- [ ] CHK005 Are feature toggles or progressive rollout requirements documented if applicable?

## Frontend Validation

- [ ] CHK006 Are component responsibilities clearly defined and scoped without overlap?
- [ ] CHK007 Are state management boundaries between components explicitly specified?
- [ ] CHK008 Are form validation rules and error feedback requirements documented?
- [ ] CHK009 Are navigation flows and routing requirements defined for all entry points?
- [ ] CHK010 Are visual feedback requirements (loading spinners, skeletons, toasts) specified?
- [ ] CHK011 Is dark mode behavior defined for all new UI surfaces?
- [ ] CHK012 Are component reuse expectations consistent with the existing design system?

## Responsive UX Validation

- [ ] CHK013 Are responsive breakpoint requirements defined (mobile / tablet / desktop)?
- [ ] CHK014 Are touch target sizes and mobile usability requirements specified?
- [ ] CHK015 Are layout shift and content reflow behaviors defined across breakpoints?
- [ ] CHK016 Is the mobile navigation pattern specified and consistent with existing patterns?
- [ ] CHK017 Are visual hierarchy requirements consistent across all screen sizes?

## Backend Validation

- [ ] CHK018 Are API routes organized under a consistent, documented URL structure?
- [ ] CHK019 Are service layer responsibilities separated from route handlers in requirements?
- [ ] CHK020 Are input validation and sanitization requirements defined for all endpoints?
- [ ] CHK021 Is authentication/authorization scope defined for each protected route?
- [ ] CHK022 Are controller, service, and repository boundaries explicitly specified?
- [ ] CHK023 Are logging requirements defined for critical operations and errors?
- [ ] CHK024 Is the feature's backend coupling to other modules documented and justified?

## Database Validation

- [ ] CHK025 Are entity relationships and foreign key constraints clearly specified?
- [ ] CHK026 Are table and column naming conventions consistent with the existing schema?
- [ ] CHK027 Are migration requirements defined and reversible (up/down)?
- [ ] CHK028 Is PostgreSQL compatibility considered for all SQLite-specific constructs?
- [ ] CHK029 Are indexing requirements defined for frequently queried columns?
- [ ] CHK030 Is unnecessary data duplication avoided and documented if intentional?
- [ ] CHK031 Are soft delete or archival patterns consistent with existing data strategy?

## API Validation

- [ ] CHK032 Are request/response contracts documented with field types and constraints?
- [ ] CHK033 Are HTTP status codes specified consistently across all endpoint scenarios?
- [ ] CHK034 Are error response shapes consistent with the project's API error format?
- [ ] CHK035 Are pagination, filtering, and sorting requirements defined where applicable?
- [ ] CHK036 Are API versioning or backward-compatibility considerations documented?
- [ ] CHK037 Is the frontend/backend decoupling preserved — no business logic in API layer?

## Error Handling

- [ ] CHK038 Are error boundaries defined for all async operations on the frontend?
- [ ] CHK039 Are API error responses mapped to user-facing messages in requirements?
- [ ] CHK040 Are network failure and timeout scenarios addressed in the spec?
- [ ] CHK041 Are fallback behaviors specified for all critical data dependencies?
- [ ] CHK042 Are validation error formats consistent between client and server requirements?

## Authentication & Security

- [ ] CHK043 Are authentication requirements applied consistently to all protected resources?
- [ ] CHK044 Are authorization rules (role/permission boundaries) explicitly defined?
- [ ] CHK045 Are sensitive data handling and storage requirements documented?
- [ ] CHK046 Are CSRF, XSS, or injection risks addressed in API and form requirements?
- [ ] CHK047 Are session expiry and token refresh behaviors specified?

## Performance & Scalability

- [ ] CHK048 Are performance targets quantified (response time, load time) for critical paths?
- [ ] CHK049 Are caching strategies defined for expensive queries or repeated API calls?
- [ ] CHK050 Are N+1 query risks identified and addressed in data access requirements?
- [ ] CHK051 Are scalability assumptions documented (expected data volume, user load)?
- [ ] CHK052 Are bundle size or lazy-loading requirements specified for frontend assets?

## Accessibility Basics

- [ ] CHK053 Are ARIA roles and labels defined for all interactive UI elements?
- [ ] CHK054 Are keyboard navigation requirements specified for all interactive components?
- [ ] CHK055 Are color contrast requirements met for all new text and UI surfaces?
- [ ] CHK056 Are focus management requirements defined for modals, drawers, and overlays?

## Documentation

- [ ] CHK057 Are architectural decisions documented with rationale (ADR or inline)?
- [ ] CHK058 Are non-obvious implementation choices explained in comments or docs?
- [ ] CHK059 Is the public API surface (endpoints, hooks, components) documented?
- [ ] CHK060 Are environment variables and configuration requirements documented?
- [ ] CHK061 Are known limitations or deferred scope items noted for future reference?

## Analytics & AI Readiness *(optional)*

- [ ] CHK062 Are user interaction events identified for future analytics instrumentation?
- [ ] CHK063 Are data points generated by this feature consistent with the analytics schema?
- [ ] CHK064 Are habit, goal, or productivity metrics structured for future AI consumption?
- [ ] CHK065 Are event naming conventions consistent with the existing tracking strategy?
- [ ] CHK066 Is the feature's data output compatible with future ML/AI pipeline requirements?

## Learning & Architecture Review

- [ ] CHK067 Was the architectural reasoning for key decisions documented?
- [ ] CHK068 Were new concepts or patterns encountered during implementation noted?
- [ ] CHK069 Was unnecessary complexity avoided — is the simplest correct solution used?
- [ ] CHK070 Is the implementation understandable without prior context for future review?
- [ ] CHK071 Were any constitution principles stretched or violated, and was it justified?

## Implementation Quality

- [ ] CHK072 Is naming (variables, functions, routes, tables) consistent and self-describing?
- [ ] CHK073 Is the implementation free of overengineering relative to current requirements?
- [ ] CHK074 Is temporary technical debt documented with a rationale and follow-up note?
- [ ] CHK075 Are dependencies introduced minimal and justified?
- [ ] CHK076 Would this implementation be maintainable by a solo developer returning after 3 months?

## Deployment & Environment Readiness *(optional)*

- [ ] CHK077 Are all required environment variables documented and safe for production?
- [ ] CHK078 Are configuration differences between development and production addressed?
- [ ] CHK079 Is the feature stable enough to be shipped without breaking existing flows?
- [ ] CHK080 Are any database migrations safe to run in a live environment?

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add inline findings or comments directly below relevant items
- Link to relevant spec sections, ADRs, or related documentation
- Optional sections (Analytics & AI Readiness, Deployment) can be skipped if not applicable
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference and traceability
